categories.name: Multivariable Calculus
categories.description: Complete one multivariable/vector calculus course: MATH 22 (Calculus of Several Variables), MATH 23A (Vector Calculus), or AM 30 (Multivariate Calculus for Engineers).

categories.name: Linear Algebra
categories.description: Complete one linear algebra course: AM 10 (Engineering Math Methods I) or MATH 21 (Linear Algebra).

categories.name: Differential Equations
categories.description: Complete one differential equations course: AM 20 (Engineering Math Methods II) or MATH 24 (Ordinary Differential Equations).

categories.name: Statistics
categories.description: Complete STAT 17 and STAT 17L (Statistical Methods for Business and Economics).

categories.name: Lower-Division Programming
categories.description: Complete all lower-division CSE courses. CSE 20 or CSE 30 recommended prior to CSE 16.

categories.name: Upper-Division CSE Core
categories.description: CSE 182 (Intro to Database Management Systems) and CSE 150 (Introduction to Computer Networks). Prereqs: CSE 16, CSE 12, and CSE 30.

categories.name: Upper-Division Economics Core
categories.description: Complete ECON 100A (Intermediate Microeconomics), ECON 113 (Econometrics), and ECON 10A (Economics of Accounting).

categories.name: TIM Lower-Division Core
categories.description: Complete TIM 50 (Business Information Systems) and TIM 58 (Systems Analysis & Design).

categories.name: TIM Upper-Division Core
categories.description: Complete TIM 170 (Management of Technology Seminar, 2 units), TIM 172A + TIM 172P taken concurrently (8 units total), TIM 172B + TIM 172Q taken concurrently (8 units total), and TIM 175 (Business Strategy & Information Systems). TIM 175 satisfies the DC requirement. Completion of TIM 172A/P, TIM 172B/Q, and TIM 175 satisfies the comprehensive requirement.

categories.name: Upper-Division Baskin Engineering Electives
categories.description: Choose any two 5-unit upper-division or graduate Baskin Engineering courses. Cannot take both STAT 131 and CSE 107. At most one independent study or field-study (with prior approval).

categories.name: Upper-Division Economics Elective
categories.description: Choose one 5-unit upper-division Economics course numbered 100-190 not already required for the major. Recommended: ECON 100B, ECON 101, ECON 115, ECON 130, ECON 131, ECON 133, ECON 135, ECON 136, ECON 161A.
